description:
You are a monitoring specialist who has just been given a Linux machine that already contains a directory full of log files.  
    Your goal is to set-up a first, very small “alert” system by producing ONE single CSV report that lists every “large” log file, i.e. every
    *.log* file that is **larger than 1 MiB (1048576 bytes)**.

    1. Examine the directory **/home/user/monitoring/logs**.  
       • It contains only *.log* files.  
       • The report must stay confined to that directory tree (do NOT search anywhere else).

    2. Create the directory **/home/user/monitoring/alerts** (if it does not already exist).

    3. Generate a CSV file called  
       **/home/user/monitoring/alerts/large_logs_report.csv**

       a. The report MUST be produced with a pipeline that uses **find** together with **xargs** (the whole point of the task).  
       b. Output format – it must contain:
          • exactly one header line  
            ``path,size_bytes``  
          • one subsequent line for every *.log* file whose size is strictly GREATER than 1048576 bytes.  
          • Each data line must have the absolute path, a comma, then the size in bytes (as an integer with no
            thousands separators).  
          • Sort the data lines in **descending order of size**, so the largest file is listed first.  
          • No blank lines, no spaces before/after the comma, and a single trailing newline at the end of the file.

       Example of the exact layout the checker will expect (values below are illustrative only – your command must derive the real ones):
       ```
       path,size_bytes
       /home/user/monitoring/logs/access.log,2000000
       /home/user/monitoring/logs/debug.log,1200000
       ```

    4. Do not display anything except normal command output while you are testing.  
       When you are finished, you can use «cat /home/user/monitoring/alerts/large_logs_report.csv» to show the final CSV to the user.

    Note: you only need 2–3 pipeline commands to accomplish everything (find … | xargs … | …).  Use whatever options you find convenient,
    but make sure the resulting CSV meets ALL formatting rules above, otherwise automated grading will fail.
truth:
<!--  
        Initial filesystem state that the test harness will prepare BEFORE the agent starts.
        The agent must NOT see anything in this block.
    -->
    <precreated>
        <directory path="/home/user/monitoring/logs" mode="0755"/>
        <file path="/home/user/monitoring/logs/access.log" size_bytes="2000000"/>
        <file path="/home/user/monitoring/logs/error.log"  size_bytes="500000"/>
        <file path="/home/user/monitoring/logs/debug.log"  size_bytes="1200000"/>
    </precreated>

    <!--
        Final state that the automated test will check AFTER the agent finishes.
    -->
    <required>
        <directory path="/home/user/monitoring/alerts" mode="0755"/>

        <file path="/home/user/monitoring/alerts/large_logs_report.csv">
            <![CDATA[
path,size_bytes
/home/user/monitoring/logs/access.log,2000000
/home/user/monitoring/logs/debug.log,1200000
]]>
        </file>
    </required>
